summary: Half-Step opener, soaring jams, standout Supplication & Foolish Heart
blurb: The set opens with a powerful Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo that launches the night. Highlights include a scorching Supplication, fiery Foolish Heart and a soaring Stella Blue climax, while the dual drummers lock in tight grooves through Corinna/Space.
review: May 22, 1993 at Shoreline Amphitheatre captured one of the Dead’s late‑era high points. Jerry Garcia rides the opening Half‑Step with his trademark lyrical tone, turning it into a springboard for an inspired second set that features a blistering Supplication and a heartfelt Foolish Heart. Vince Welnick’s keyboards add a jazzy, Brent‑esque texture throughout, especially on Foolish Heart, while Phil Lesh provides a melodic bass foundation that locks in with the dual‑drummer groove of Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann during the Corinna‑Space excursion. The set closes on an emotional Stella Blue that showcases Jerry’s soaring lead, making this show a must‑listen for fans who love tight jams and emotive moments alike.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are strongly positive, repeatedly praising the Half‑Step jam, Supplication, Foolish Heart and Jerry’s tone; a third recording is mixed but still highlights Supplication. Consistent acclaim across multiple sources warrants a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent DAT‑derived soundboard capture with a crisp, well‑balanced mix; includes the full, unedited performance.

key_highlights: Powerful Half‑Step opener that fuels the entire night
Scorching Supplication and fiery Foolish Heart performances
Dual‑drummer groove shines on Corinna/Space and drives the set
Emotional Stella Blue closing with soaring guitar

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is repeatedly lauded—on Half‑Step, Stagger Lee and the soaring ending of Stella Blue he sounds on fire, delivering lyrical solos that drive the set.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh locks in with Jerry during the Half‑Step jam, providing a solid, melodic bass foundation that underpins the band’s high energy.
band_performance.Bob: Weir contributes solid rhythm work and adds weight to Supplication, complementing the overall groove.
band_performance.Vince: Welnick shines on Foolish Heart with jazzy, Brent‑style textures and adds rich keyboard color throughout the set.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming is highlighted in the Corinna/Space segment, delivering a driving rhythmic backbone that propels the improvisations.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s drums are prominent, especially at the start of Corinna, reinforcing the dual‑drummer dynamic.
